# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Envoy configuration with HTTP filters for request processing. Define filter chains including router filter for traffic routing. Set up retry policies and timeout configurations for backend connections. This is for our service mesh data plane.
# Category: envoy_authz_filter_missing

apiVersion: "networking.istio.io/v1alpha3"
kind: "EnvoyFilter"
metadata: 
 name: 'request-filter'   # Name of the filter, it will be used as a prefix for all resource names in Istio configuration files and also to identify resources created by this Envoy Filter at runtime. It should not contain slashes (/) or dots (.); these are reserved characters within YAML
spec: 
 configPatches:   # List of patches that will be applied on the filter's current state before it is used for routing/communication with an upstream service in a request chain, and after all other filters have been processed. The patch should contain either addition or removal operations (additions are added to resources; removals remove from those).
 - op: "remove"   # Operation type can be 'replace' if you want the filter replaced by another one with same name in Envoy configuration, otherwise it is an add operation and will replace existing resource. 
   path: "/envoy/static_resources/xds_rbac_configs/"    # Path to remove from current state (can be a full or partial match). For example '/a' would mean removing all 'a'. The trailing slash is not allowed in the middle of paths. 
 - value: "*"   # Value can either contain an exact string, which will replace existing resource at path; if it contains glob pattern (starting with *), then that part of configuration file should be removed from Envoy's current state and replaced by new one provided in the patch section for this filter.
type: STRICT_DNS  # Type can either contain an exact string, which will replace existing resource at path; if it contains glob pattern (starting with *), then that part of configuration file should be removed from Envoy's current state and replaced by new one provided in the patch section for this filter.
